summary: Add a walkFeed primitive, make 'clips list --all' actually drain every page, and give sync client-side incremental clips sync via early-stop on created_at. Extends sync-warn-explicit-since-only: the warning now lives in the no-timestamp-field branch.
reason: 'clips list --all' was an inert flag (declared, never read) that returned only the first page on a ~1000-track account. Sync re-drained the entire library every run because no Suno endpoint declares a server temporal-filter param. The clips feed is a reverse-chron cursor feed with per-record created_at, so incremental works via client-side early-stop: walk newest->oldest and stop once a page's oldest record predates the boundary (last_synced_at minus a 5m clock-skew margin on the automatic path, or an explicit --since). Workspaces have no payload timestamp and stay full-pull. walkFeed guards against limit==0 disabling its short-page check and against a sticky (non-advancing) cursor.
